result.stderr:
GitSMimeSign 1.6.4+9940d2f9b8
Copyright (c) 2021 Glenn Watson

  -u, --local-user             Use USER-ID to sign

  -b, --detached-sign          Make a detached signature

  -a, --armor                  Create ASCII armored output

  --status-fd                  Write special status strings to the specified
                               file descriptor

  -t, --timestamp-authority    (Default: http://timestamp.digicert.com) A URL to
                               the RFC 3161 time stamp authority.

  --include-certs              (Default: -2) This option overrides the command
                               line option --include-certs. A value of -2
                               includes all certificates except for the root
                               certificate, -1 includes all certificates, 0 does
                               not include any certificates, 1 includes only the
                               signers certificate and all other positive values
                               include up to value certificates starting with
                               the signer cert. Default is -2.

  --keyid-format               (Default: long) Select how to display key IDs.
                               'none' does not show the key ID at all but shows
                               the fingerprint in a separate line. 'short' is
                               the traditional 8-character key ID. 'long' is the
                               more accurate (but less convenient) 16-character
                               key ID. Add an '0x' to either to include an '0x'
                               at the beginning of the key ID, as in 0x99242560.

  --list-keys                  List all the keys available on the system.

  --verify                     Verifies a signature.

  -s, --sign                   Sign a object and make a signature.

  --help                       Display this help screen.

  --version                    Display version information.

  fileName (pos. 0)            The file name.

options.description: This option overrides the command line option --include-certs. A value of -2 includes all certificates except for the root certificate, -1 includes all certificates, 0 does not include any certificates, 1 includes only the signers certificate and all other positive values include up to value certificates starting with the signer cert. Default is -2.

Select how to display key IDs. 'none' does not show the key ID at all but shows the fingerprint in a separate line. 'short' is the traditional 8-character key ID. 'long' is the more accurate (but less convenient) 16-character key ID. Add an '0x' to either to include an '0x' at the beginning of the key ID, as in 0x99242560.
List all the keys available on the system.
Sign a object and make a signature.
Write special status strings to the specified file descriptor
A URL to the RFC 3161 time stamp authority.
